text:
Once upon a time in the quaint village of Hawthorne, there lived a reclusive writer named Eliot Lockwood. Famous for his intricate tales of mystery and romance, Eliot spent his days in his cozy study peering out through his large glass window, gazing at the serene forest beyond.

It was a sunny autumn day when Eliot decided it was time for his annual Halloween festivity. He'd always created a unique collection: an anthology of ghost stories that wouldn't frighten the living, but invite them into his imaginative world.

Dressed in a cloak and wielding a wooden spoon as his scepter, Eliot embarked on his costume extravaganza. His haunted house was ready—filled with whispering portraits, flickering candles, and a few eerie sounds.

As he circulated his ghost stories, people couldn't help but laugh—the ghost stories had an unexpected charm. His favorite anecdote involved a ghostly ballroom where dancing specters accidentally filled the air with polkas.
